2605.23420.title: Naturalistic measure of social norms alignment
2605.23420.paper_summary: Social norms reflect shared expectations on acceptable behavior. Measuring social norms alignment remains challenging, with existing approaches typically relying on artificial closed-form evaluations such as multiple-choice questionnaires or measuring agreement with predefined statements. In the context of this work, social norms alignment refers to measuring an agreement between solutions with respect to the social problem or dilemma. We propose a framework for measuring social norm alignment in naturalistic, free-form settings through solution matching. The framework enables us to measure alignment between any two dilemma responses e.g., LLMs to a human, LLMs to LLMs, or human to human. We introduce two metrics: stated and explicit agreement accuracy, and construct a dataset of 3k non-trivial social dilemmas in Danish. All dilemmas are assigned reference solutions derived from three panelists, who serve as culturally grounded judges. We evaluate the agreement of several LLMs and human responses in an interaction setup that resembles natural user-model conversations. Our results show that the proposed metrics produce consistent model rankings and reveal variation in agreement across different types of dilemmas, with higher agreement observed for topics such as neighbor conflicts and shared living situations. Overall, our work introduces a dataset and evaluation framework for studying culturally grounded social reasoning in naturalistic open-ended conversations.
2605.23420.paper_summary_zh: 社會規範反映了對可接受行為的共同期望。測量社會規範的一致性仍然具有挑戰性，現有的方法通常依賴於人工的封閉式評估，例如多選題問卷或測量對預定陳述的同意程度。在本研究的背景下，社會規範的一致性是指測量解決方案在社會問題或困境方面的協議。我們提出了一個框架，用於通過解決方案匹配在自然主義、自由形式的環境中測量社會規範的一致性。該框架使我們能夠測量任何兩個困境反應之間的一致性，例如，LLMs對人類、LLMs對LLMs或人類對人類。我們引入了兩個指標：陳述和明確同意準確性，並構建了一個包含3000個非平凡社會困境的丹麥語數據集。所有困境都分配了來自三位小組成員的參考解決方案，他們作為文化根植的評判者。我們在一個類似於自然用戶模型對話的互動設置中評估了幾個LLMs和人類反應的一致性。我們的結果顯示，所提出的指標產生了一致的模型排名，並揭示了不同類型困境之間的一致性變化，對於鄰里衝突和共享生活情況等主題觀察到更高的一致性。總體而言，我們的工作引入了一個數據集和評估框架，以研究自然主義開放式對話中的文化根植社會推理。
2605.23420.author: Yevhen Kostiuk et.al.
2605.23420.authors: Yevhen Kostiuk, Kenneth Enevoldsen, Peter Bjerregaard Vahlstrup, Márton Kardos, Kristoffer Nielbo
